content.claim: Roma students face substantially higher early school-leaving rates (58.7% vs. 9.3% non-Roma in 2023) in least-developed regions and villages, indicating ethnic/regional equity gaps distinct from and extending beyond age-12 gimnázium selection structures
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Early school-leaving includes dropout before secondary selection age; does not isolate impact of 6/8-year track design on Roma equity
OECD acknowledgment of Roma access as separate lever does not quantify its magnitude relative to tracking-structure effects
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
